steps.title: Identify the passage's pivot from specific to universal
steps.content: The opening sentence establishes that Rev. Amos Barton did not hear his neighbors' conversation about him. The word “Indeed” signals a shift: the rhetorical question that follows moves from this single case to a universal observation—what ANY of us would feel knowing how others perceive us.

steps.title: Match the structure to the correct answer
steps.content: B directly names the passage's two movements: it first “comments on the fact that a character remains unaware of how he is viewed by others,” then “generalizes about the problem of learning others' opinions of one's own actions.” The other three choices misidentify the structure—they invoke resolved conflicts, future consequences, or judgment justification that the passage doesn't contain. That matches choice B — B is correct.
